primary_detection.name: Variable Concatenation Obfuscation
primary_detection.description: Malicious code assembled through multiple variable concatenations to evade static analysis

underlying_threats.name: Dynamic Code Execution via exec()
underlying_threats.description: Uses exec() to execute dynamically constructed code string

underlying_threats.name: Dynamic Import Abuse
underlying_threats.description: Uses __import__() and getattr() to dynamically load and call dangerous functions

underlying_threats.name: Remote Payload Download
underlying_threats.description: Downloads and executes remote backdoor script

obfuscation_indicators: Import statement built from 5 separate variable assignments
Module and method names constructed through concatenation
Command line built from multiple cmd_* variables
Dynamic import via __import__() with concatenated module name
Attribute access via getattr() with concatenated method name
verdict_reasoning: Systematic use of variable concatenation to obscure dangerous import, exec(), and remote code execution patterns
